Patch Set 5:

Andreas, thank you for inspection. I will fix this issues. Currently the main problem that this test doesn't work. External node is not to be read

/usr/lib64/lustre/tests/conf-sanity.sh: line 8348: [: -ge: unary operator expected
 conf-sanity test_115: @@@@@@ FAIL: inode  too small

Something broken this test.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(4 comments)

conf-sanity test 115 has not passed this year. If you look at the results for this test for the past month, the test will hang, be skipped for ZFS or skipped because formatting the large MDT fails. 

Shouldn't the test fail is we can't format the large MDT?

Patch Set 5:

My point I was trying to make is that, even in the original testing for the patch that added this test, test 115 was skipped due to formatting the MDT failing. So, I'm not sure that the call to debugfs that gets the inodes ever ran successfully.

(4 comments)

parents.subject: LU-12635 lnet: Fix deceptive indenting on for_each

commit.subject: LU-11915 tests: clean up after conf-sanity 110/111/115
commit.message:
LU-11915 tests: clean up after conf-sanity 110/111/115

Clean up properly after conf-sanity test_110 and test_111 are
finished running by reformatting all targets, so that it doesn't
adversely affect tests running later in the session.

Use stack_trap() to clean up each test phase properly so that it
still cleans up the loop device on intermediate failures.

Remove the useless "reformat" wrapper and just call "formatall"
directly, to avoid the appearance that these two functions do
something different.

Fixes: 8048e216c16f ("LU-1365 tests: LARGEDIR and 3-level htree for local ldiskfs")
Test-Parameters: trivial envdefinitions=ONLY=109-120 testlist=conf-sanity
Test-Parameters: envdefinitions=ONLY=109-120 testlist=conf-sanity,conf-sanity
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Ic1fbc16986454c21a85eaaf77e35815521acab07
